I brushed on this earlier that you did no research on the lead. This is a mistake that is made with me and my company almost every time that we hit interested in a product or service more often than not. Okay, whether I'm buying a piece of software for follow up for a CRM or whether I'm trying to buy a watch for my wife or a car for the company or whether I'm trying to get one of my many apartment buildings a plumbing situation or a new roof or I'm looking for a dentist for this cracked tooth that I have, nobody takes the time to look at who I am. It's so easy today with the internet to use the internet the way your customer is but now to reverse this and use the internet on your customer. Use Google, okay? First thing I'm going to do is go in and Google Grant Cardone. Who is he? Who is this guy Grant Cardone or the name of the company? Who owns the company? Who's the decision maker in the company? I can pick up pictures, locations, maps, mission statements, things that they have accomplished in the community. Look, I'm going to find out everything. If the person doesn't exist on the internet then you got to start wondering what kind of customer is this? Is this customer going to be a problem for us? If you exist in real life, you exist on the internet. I can find out something about you. I can go to your Facebook page, your LinkedIn account, your Twitter account. I can look and find something if I'm wanting to turn this lead. For those of you out there who say these leads are just no good, it's because you're not doing research. Do the research. Find out who you're dealing with. So that the call or the email could go like this. Let's say I was shopping for roofs for an apartment deal in Charlotte. Could the roof for now say, Mr. Cardone, I've been a fan of yours for years? Two other companies give me bids and the third guy says, I've been a fan of your books for years Mr. Cardone and I look forward to the opportunity. Oh, by the way, I'm going to be in Miami on Thursday. Could I get time from you around two o'clock? So what am I doing here? I'm pushing the appointment up. I used research. I didn't just give a compliment, but now I'm moving into sales by pushing the appointment up by offering a personal visit. This is powerful. That's what I'm telling you. This is not just about leads. It's not just about the internet. These are real people. These are real people, real human beings. And these companies that are hitting you are filled with what? Human beings. The old adage business to business. I hear people say B2B, business to business. There's no such thing as business to business. There's people in every company. Whether the company's Ernst Young and there's 146,000 employees, remember this. Ernst Young is not a business. It is a company of people and people make decisions. And the more you can research the people that you're talking to that were leads, the more you can admire them, edify them, have a connection with them, create a relationship by doing research and then by offering to speed up this process of service, the better off you're going to be. Don't neglect the research. It's an extra step. Just don't neglect it.
